pool_structure.note: Replaces 4-box CENTURY. Passive pool was inert (K4=0).

fire.description: Sigmoid ignition x unimodal fuel response

cold_threshold.reference: Jolly et al. 2005, Global Change Biology 11:619-632

evapotranspiration.description: Hargreaves PET + Zhang Budyko + LAI partition
evapotranspiration.note: Grid-scale ET formulation. Not a direct C++ patch but a recommended replacement for Soil_Canopy_evap.
references: Zhang et al. 2001, Water Resources Research 37:701-708

mortality.description: Carbon balance mortality sigmoid and growth respiration

m2.justification: Caps maximum carbon-starvation mortality at 2/yr. m2=10 implied cohorts die 10x/yr, faster than any observed die-off event. McDowell et al. 2011 review shows carbon starvation operates on month-to-year timescales.

m3.justification: Softens the carbon balance sigmoid. m3=20 gives half-life of 50 days at cbr_bar=0, killing any tree with marginal carbon balance within weeks. Real trees survive bad years using stored carbon reserves in sapwood and starch. m3=5 gives half-life of 4.5 years at cbr=0, allowing boreal vegetation with short growing seasons to persist.
m3.reference: Hoch et al. 2003, Plant Cell & Environment 26:1067-1081

growth_resp.justification: Restored to ED 2001 value. 0.33 is the standard literature estimate for growth respiration as a fraction of NPP (Waring et al. 1998). The increase to 0.5 in ED v3.0 is undocumented.
growth_resp.reference: Waring et al. 1998, Advances in Ecological Research 28:113-175

mortality.expected_effect: Extends vegetation coverage into high-latitude cells where short growing seasons produce low but positive annual carbon balance. Directly addresses the 22% vegetation coverage gap identified in this study.
